gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that literal **{...} dict unpacks bypassed the duplicate-key guard. The scanner now flattens literal unpacked dicts in source order, ignores dynamic unpacks, and avoids duplicate diagnostics for nested literal dict nodes that were already flattened. Final GPT-5.5 review was clean.
scope: PR evidence and release evidence maintainability
verification: find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Added an AST-based governance helper that reports duplicate literal dictionary keys before Python can silently keep the last value.
Included literal **{...} dict unpack keys in source-order duplicate detection while ignoring dynamic keys and dynamic unpacks.

Added regressions for ordinary duplicate literal keys, duplicate integer keys, literal unpack duplicates, dynamic unpack ignore behavior, and one-report diagnostics for duplicates inside literal unpack dicts.
Added a repository-wide source scan over src/ and tests/ so JSON/evidence schema helpers cannot accidentally repeat literal fields.
why: Release, PR, and Databricks evidence records are schema-heavy dictionaries; catching duplicate literal keys in source keeps the open-source package from shipping silent overwritten fields in its evidence and benchmark contracts.
